# HumanlabsLinkSDK (iOS)

> Native Swift SDK for HumanlabsLink — an open-source mobile attribution and deep link platform. Handles Universal Links, deferred deep linking (install attribution), in-app event tracking with offline queue, and revenue tracking. Zero external dependencies. The mobile SDK does NOT create links — link creation is server-side only (see "Creating links" below). `baseURL` defaults to production, so most apps pass no config.

## Requirements

- iOS 16.0+
- Swift 5.9+
- Xcode 15.0+

## Installation

CocoaPods:

```ruby
# Podfile
pod 'HumanlabsLinkSDK'
```

Then run `pod install`.

> SwiftPM is not supported: the package lives in a monorepo subdirectory and SwiftPM only resolves a package from a repository root. Use CocoaPods.

## Swift Types

```swift
struct HumanlabsLinkConfig {
    let baseURL: URL                     // default: https://link.humanlabs.world (production)
    let appToken: String?                // Cloud only — recommended; enables organic-install attribution
    let debug: Bool                      // default: false
    let attributionWindowHours: Int      // default: 168 (7 days), range: 1-2160
}

struct DeepLinkData {
    let shortCode: String
    let iosURL: String?
    let androidURL: String?
    let webURL: String?
    /// UTM parameters: source/medium/campaign/term/content
    let utmParameters: UTMParameters?
    /// Custom key-value parameters delivered to the app. Includes link-fixed
    /// `deepLinkParameters` plus passthrough click params (referral_code, ph_did).
    let customParameters: [String: String]?
    /// In-app destination path (e.g., "/product/123")
    let deepLinkPath: String?
    /// Custom URI scheme (e.g., "myapp")
    let appScheme: String?
    let clickedAt: Date?
    let linkId: String?
}

struct UTMParameters {
    let source: String?
    let medium: String?
    let campaign: String?
    let term: String?
    let content: String?
}

struct InstallResponse {
    let installId: String
    let attributed: Bool
    /// Attribution confidence score (0-100)
    let confidenceScore: Int
    /// Matched fingerprint factors (e.g., ["ip", "user_agent", "timezone"])
    let matchedFactors: [String]
    let deepLinkData: DeepLinkData?
}

/// All errors thrown by the SDK
enum HumanlabsLinkError: Error {
    case notInitialized
    case alreadyInitialized
    case invalidConfiguration(String)
    case networkError(Error)
    case invalidResponse(statusCode: Int?, message: String?)
    case decodingError(Error)
    case encodingError(Error)
    case invalidEventData(String)
    case invalidDeepLinkURL(String)
}
```

## Creating links

The mobile SDK does not create links. Create them server-side with
`@humanlabs-kr/link-server-sdk` (npm), then deliver the short URL to the app.
Custom values ride along via the link's `deepLinkParameters` or click-URL query
(`?utm_campaign=…&referral_code=…&ph_did=…`) and arrive in `DeepLinkData.customParameters` / `.utmParameters`.

## SDK API

The SDK uses a singleton: `HumanlabsLink.shared`. All async methods use Swift async/await.

```swift
import HumanlabsLinkSDK
```

### initialize(config:) async throws -> InstallResponse

Initialize the SDK, report the install, and return attribution data. Must be called before any other method. `baseURL` defaults to production, so the config is optional.

```swift
// Simplest — production defaults
let response = try await HumanlabsLink.shared.initialize(config: HumanlabsLinkConfig())

// Or override (staging, debug, Cloud appToken)
let config = HumanlabsLinkConfig(
    appToken: "at_a1b2c3d4...",     // recommended for Cloud — enables organic-install attribution
    debug: true
)
let response = try await HumanlabsLink.shared.initialize(config: config)

print("Install ID:", response.installId)
print("Attributed:", response.attributed)
print("Confidence:", response.confidenceScore)
```

### onDeepLink(\_ callback:)

Register a callback for direct deep links — fires when user taps a Universal Link and the app is already installed.

```swift
HumanlabsLink.shared.onDeepLink { url, data in
    print("Deep link opened:", url)
    let ref = data?.customParameters?["referral_code"]   // passthrough click param
    if let route = data?.customParameters?["route"] {
        NavigationManager.shared.navigate(to: route)
    }
}
```

### onDeferredDeepLink(\_ callback:)

Register a callback for deferred deep links — fires on first app launch if the install was attributed to a link click.

```swift
HumanlabsLink.shared.onDeferredDeepLink { data in
    if let data = data {
        print("Attributed install from:", data.shortCode)
        let ref = data.customParameters?["referral_code"]   // e.g. referral reward
        let phDid = data.customParameters?["ph_did"]         // posthog.identify → web↔app stitching
        let campaign = data.utmParameters?.campaign
    }
}
```

**iOS deferred attribution is clipboard-based (automatic, no setup).** Apple
prohibits device fingerprinting, so on first launch the SDK reads a clipboard
hand-off token the web interstitial wrote (`hl_cid=<clickId>`) and attributes the
install deterministically. Prompt hygiene: the SDK checks for a URL with
`UIPasteboard.detectPatterns(.probableWebURL)` — which does **not** prompt — and
only reads (showing the iOS 16+ "Allow Paste" prompt) when a URL is present, so
organic installs see no prompt. If the user denies the prompt, the install stays
organic. No extra dependency or configuration required (UIKit is built in).

### handleDeepLink(url:)

Forward Universal Links to the SDK for processing. Call this from your AppDelegate or SwiftUI `.onOpenURL`.

```swift
HumanlabsLink.shared.handleDeepLink(url: url)
```

### trackEvent(name:properties:) async throws

Track in-app events. Failed sends are queued offline (max 100 events) and retried automatically.

```swift
try await HumanlabsLink.shared.trackEvent(
    name: "add_to_cart",
    properties: ["productId": "789", "price": 29.99]
)
```

### trackRevenue(amount:currency:properties:) async throws

Track revenue events. Amount must be non-negative.

```swift
try await HumanlabsLink.shared.trackRevenue(
    amount: Decimal(29.99),
    currency: "USD",
    properties: ["productId": "789", "orderId": "order_123"]
)
```

### Last-click attribution (automatic)

Every event (including screen views) is automatically stamped with the deep link that most recently opened the app — deferred install or direct re-engagement — plus an app-open `sessionId`. The newest deep-link open supersedes the previous one, and the active link persists across app restarts. Events with no preceding deep-link open are organic (session only). No code is required; the backend uses the stamp to credit in-app activity to the originating link.

### trackScreenView(name:properties:) async throws

Report a screen view for per-link screen-flow funnels. Emits a `screen_view` event (with `screen`, the previous screen as `previousScreen`, and the attribution stamp). Throws on an empty name.

```swift
// UIKit — from viewDidAppear
Task { try? await HumanlabsLink.shared.trackScreenView(name: "ProductDetail") }
```

SwiftUI — use the `.humanlabsLinkScreen(_:)` view modifier:

```swift
ProductView()
    .humanlabsLinkScreen("ProductDetail")
```

No navigation swizzling: mark the screens you care about, which keeps screen names meaningful.

### Offline Event Queue

```swift
HumanlabsLink.shared.queuedEventCount   // number of events waiting
await HumanlabsLink.shared.flushEvents() // manually flush
HumanlabsLink.shared.clearEventQueue()   // clear without sending
```

### Utility Methods

```swift
HumanlabsLink.shared.getInstallId()    // String?
HumanlabsLink.shared.getInstallData()  // DeepLinkData?
HumanlabsLink.shared.isFirstLaunch()   // Bool
HumanlabsLink.shared.clearData()       // clear all stored data
HumanlabsLink.shared.reset()           // reset to uninitialized state
```

## Universal Links Setup

1. In Xcode: target → Signing & Capabilities → + Capability → Associated Domains. Add:

```
applinks:link.humanlabs.world
```

2. Register your Apple Team ID and App Bundle ID in the backend (`apps` registry / MCP `create_app`).

3. HumanlabsLink automatically serves `/.well-known/apple-app-site-association` from the domain.

## Complete SwiftUI Example

```swift
// MyApp.swift
import SwiftUI
import HumanlabsLinkSDK

@main
struct MyApp: App {
    @StateObject private var deepLinkRouter = DeepLinkRouter()

    init() {
        Task {
            do {
                // baseURL defaults to production — pass config only to override
                let response = try await HumanlabsLink.shared.initialize(config: HumanlabsLinkConfig())

                if response.attributed {
                    print("Install attributed with confidence: \(response.confidenceScore)")
                }

                HumanlabsLink.shared.onDeferredDeepLink { data in
                    if let data = data {
                        DispatchQueue.main.async {
                            deepLinkRouter.handleDeepLink(data)
                        }
                    }
                }

                HumanlabsLink.shared.onDeepLink { url, data in
                    if let data = data {
                        DispatchQueue.main.async {
                            deepLinkRouter.handleDeepLink(data)
                        }
                    }
                }
            } catch {
                print("HumanlabsLink init failed:", error)
            }
        }
    }

    var body: some Scene {
        WindowGroup {
            ContentView()
                .environmentObject(deepLinkRouter)
                .humanlabsLinkScreen("Home") // reports a screen_view (attribution-stamped) on appear
                .onOpenURL { url in
                    HumanlabsLink.shared.handleDeepLink(url: url)
                }
        }
    }
}

// DeepLinkRouter.swift
class DeepLinkRouter: ObservableObject {
    @Published var route: String?
    @Published var referralCode: String?

    func handleDeepLink(_ data: DeepLinkData) {
        self.route = data.customParameters?["route"]
        self.referralCode = data.customParameters?["referral_code"]
    }
}
```

## Complete UIKit Example

```swift
// AppDelegate.swift
import UIKit
import HumanlabsLinkSDK

@main
class AppDelegate: UIResponder, UIApplicationDelegate {
    var window: UIWindow?

    func application(_ application: UIApplication,
                     didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {

        Task {
            let response = try await HumanlabsLink.shared.initialize(config: HumanlabsLinkConfig())

            HumanlabsLink.shared.onDeferredDeepLink { data in
                if let data = data { self.routeToContent(data) }
            }

            HumanlabsLink.shared.onDeepLink { url, data in
                if let data = data { self.routeToContent(data) }
            }
        }

        return true
    }

    func application(_ application: UIApplication,
                     continue userActivity: NSUserActivity,
                     restorationHandler: @escaping ([UIUserActivityRestoring]?) -> Void) -> Bool {
        guard userActivity.activityType == NSUserActivityTypeBrowsingWeb,
              let url = userActivity.webpageURL else { return false }
        HumanlabsLink.shared.handleDeepLink(url: url)
        return true
    }

    private func routeToContent(_ data: DeepLinkData) {
        guard let route = data.customParameters?["route"] else { return }
        DispatchQueue.main.async {
            // Navigate to the appropriate view controller
        }
    }
}
```

## Testing Deep Links

```bash
# iOS Simulator
xcrun simctl openurl booted "https://link.humanlabs.world/<appSlug>/abc123"
```

## Self-Hosted vs Cloud / staging

`baseURL` defaults to production (`https://link.humanlabs.world`). Override only to point at another instance:

```swift
let config = HumanlabsLinkConfig(baseURL: URL(string: "https://staging.example.com")!)
```

All SDK methods work identically regardless of `baseURL`.
